anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Radical Jodo Shinshu other-power: true shinjin is not a human capacity at all, but Amida's working, and self-assessment as capacity may itself be self-power calculation.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers originality and confidence. The model survives only if capacity is redefined as functional support locus rather than practitioner-owned competence.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If entrusting is not merely low agency but a denial that the practitioner owns the decisive receiving condition, then the ledger misnames a theological claim as a psychological capacity. A similar Dzogchen objection says recognition is not a practitioner's skill but primordial awareness recognized through introduction.

claim_units: The needed practitioner support is not one generic minimum self, but a five-column ledger: receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration.
A path can reduce one capacity load only by raising another or relocating the load to a teacher, vow, community, text, or post-insight discipline.
Effortful Buddhist practice places high load on agency, memory, and reflexivity before insight.
Other-power practice lowers self-powered agency but raises or relocates receiving, hearing, entrusting, and freedom from calculation.
Direct-introduction practice lowers production at the threshold but raises recognition, teacher relation, and stabilization.
The model predicts distinct failure modes when a load is absent, inflated, or placed in the wrong locus.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Identity-transforming modalities such as trauma therapy, psychedelic therapy, exposure therapy, Internal Family Systems, and critical pedagogy should differ from ordinary skills training by having stronger trust-building, screening, memory supports, facilitator authority, and integration practices. Low-agency modalities should compensate with higher relational containment and post-session integration.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Any method that transforms identity must place functional load somewhere: client agency, trust in therapist or method, memory of practice, reflexive self-observation, group support, or integration after the decisive session.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Identity-transforming methods require no more scaffolding than ordinary skill acquisition, or failures do not track where agency, trust, memory, reflexivity, and integration are placed.

audits.exact_claim: Awakening paths should be compared by where they place five functional loads: receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration. Effort, gift, direct introduction, and practice-realization do not remove capacity requirements, they redistribute them to the practitioner, teacher, vow, community, text, present act, or post-insight discipline.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Right-effort and gradual-path texts should emphasize intention, repetition, memory, correction, and ethical maintenance. Other-power and direct-introduction texts should not show zero safeguards, but should show safeguards around doubt, false trust, teacher qualification, distraction, antinomian misuse, recognition, and stabilization.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practice manuals should show patterned safeguards matching their load distribution.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coded comparison of SN 45.8, Shinran letters and Tannisho, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho materials, Dogen Bendowa, and contemporary lineage manuals.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Manuals show no relation between path type and safeguard profile, or safeguards track only institution, genre, or teacher personality.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: The same practitioner reports that vipassana authorizes effortful observation and memory of instructions, Pure Land authorizes relinquishing calculative self-power, Dzogchen authorizes recognition and non-distraction, and Soto practice-realization authorizes full enactment without future-gaining.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report different permitted self-capacities when switching methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with practitioners trained in at least two of Theravada, Jodo Shinshu, Dzogchen or Mahamudra, Soto Zen, Advaita, and Sufism.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports are explained better by temperament, teacher style, or retreat setting, with no stable method-specific capacity shift.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Gift practice distorted into agency produces anxious self-checking or pride. Direct introduction without stabilization produces reification, distraction, or antinomian confidence. Effort practice without memory or reflexivity produces mechanical repetition or bypassing.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Failures should follow the misplaced load, not merely practice intensity.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Re-coding adverse meditation reports, teacher case notes, and practitioner interviews with ledger fields.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Failure types do not correlate with load placement, or all traditions show the same generic adverse patterns after controlling for trauma, isolation, sleep, and teacher support.

literature_search_queries: Jack Engler being somebody before nobody ego strength no-self meditation
Shinran Other Power self power calculation entrusting practitioner capacity
Dzogchen pointing-out recognition stabilization safeguards direct introduction
Buddhist Upaya different practitioners predispositions capacity vehicles
functional self no-self meditation self-pattern agency ownership
spiritual bypassing trauma no-self mindfulness Engler Welwood

near_neighbors.difference: The current idea implements that recommendation and adds a sharper gift-effort-direct-introduction comparison with a load relocation rule.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Decisive internal reduction. This is an extension of a prior Lumenary result, not a new discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact internal prior. The audit already recommended recasting the minimum-self thesis as graded variables: receiving capacity, agency, memory, reflexivity, and post-insight integration.
near_neighbors.source: Lumenary originality audit, Every Path Needs What Its Teaching Dissolves, reviews/originality/2026-05-26-every-path-needs-what-its-teaching-dissolves-daeecdbd22658370.json

near_neighbors.difference: Engler does not split the requirement into five scored load categories or compare Other Power, right effort, Dzogchen, and practice-realization as load distributions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The basic insight that self-dissolving practice needs functional capacities is known.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the prerequisite claim. Engler argues that meditation and no-self practice require ego strengths such as self-observation and affect tolerance.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Engler, Being Somebody and Being Nobody, discussed in Psychoanalysis and Buddhism, and practitioner-facing summary at https://mindfulfeeling.ca/on-being-somebody-and-being-nobody/

near_neighbors.difference: The current idea shifts from metaphysical personhood to practice architecture across multiple traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the continuity problem, moderate preservation for the cross-tradition ledger.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close historical near-neighbor. Pudgalavada debates already expose the pressure between no-self doctrine and the need to account for karma, rebirth, liberation, and lived personhood.
near_neighbors.source: Pudgalavada Buddhist Philosophy, Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://iep.utm.edu/pudgalavada-buddhist-philosophy/

near_neighbors.difference: Upaya is a Buddhist hermeneutic and pedagogy model. It does not score receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration across gift and effort traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The capacity-sensitive pedagogy structure is known, but the ledger is a useful reformulation.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close structural neighbor. Upaya already treats different vehicles and teachings as suited to different practitioners, predispositions, and receptacles.
near_neighbors.source: Andrew McGarrity, Using Skilful Means Skilfully, Journal of Religious History, https://buddhism.lib.ntu.edu.tw/FULLTEXT/JR-MAG/mag204410.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The paper is primarily Buddhist and cognitive-scientific. It does not compare gift, effort, and direct introduction as capacity relocations.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction, especially for agency, integration, and self-pattern transformation claims.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close contemporary model for meditation transforming self-patterns through ethics, attention, meta-awareness, deconstruction, flexibility, and selfless integration.

near_neighbors.difference: VCE does not propose a five-column capacity ledger or predict failures by gift-effort load distribution.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for the empirical-adjacent failure and support-structure components.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close to the failure-mode and safeguard claim. VCE identifies meditation challenges, interpretive frameworks, management strategies, teacher roles, practice factors, and sense-of-self effects.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., The Varieties of Contemplative Experience, PLOS ONE, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

next_loop_instructions: Rename practitioner capacity as functional support locus when auditing Other Power, grace, and Dzogchen, so the model does not smuggle self-power into traditions that reject it.
Build the five-field ledger as an explicit coding sheet with values for practitioner-owned, teacher-held, vow-held, community-held, text-held, present-act-held, and post-insight-held load.
Code primary manuals before using broad tradition labels, beginning with SN 45.8, Tannisho, Shinran letters, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, Bendowa, and one Advaita prerequisite text.
Ask Jodo Shinshu and Dzogchen practitioners whether receiving and recognition can be called capacities without doctrinal distortion.
Do not promote above extended unless coded manuals and practitioner interviews show predictive failure patterns that Engler, Upaya, and prior Lumenary work do not already explain.
audits.novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.61 to about 0.49. The ledger is useful and testable, but it largely extends the audited Lumenary minimum-self thesis and known work on Engler, Upaya, Buddhist personhood, self-pattern theory, and adverse meditation contexts.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is just Engler, Upaya, adhikara, two truths, ordinary pedagogy, or Shinran's self-power versus Other Power distinction in new vocabulary. Pure Land or Dzogchen teachers say the model misdescribes their decisive moment by making it a human capacity.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the five fields to predict real differences in screening, warnings, teacher dependence, post-insight integration, and error patterns across traditions better than the simpler effort-versus-gift contrast.
questions: Is receiving or entrusting a practitioner capacity in your tradition, or is that already a self-power mistake?
Does a five-field ledger change how you would screen, sequence, warn, or verify students?
Are the proposed failure modes obvious from standard training, or does the ledger predict something you would not otherwise notice?

primary_pair: Shinran, Letter to Senshin, Collected Works of Shinran, https://shinranworks.com/letters/a-collection-of-letters-zensho-text/what-we-call-other-power/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may smuggle self-power back into Shinran by treating entrusting as a practitioner capacity. It may also psychologize Dzogchen recognition as a personal skill rather than primordial awareness recognizing itself. The safer formulation is that paths require functional support loci, not necessarily capacities owned by the practitioner.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search found very close prior art in Engler's ego-strength-before-no-self thesis, Buddhist personhood debates, Pudgalavada arguments about karma and liberation, Buddhist functional-self and self-pattern theory, Upaya as capacity-sensitive pedagogy, spiritual bypassing literature, and Lumenary's prior audited idea Every Path Needs What Its Teaching Dissolves. I did not find an exact five-column gift-effort ledger with relocation rules across Pure Land, Dzogchen, right effort, and practice-realization, but the main architecture was already specified in the prior Lumenary audit's next-loop instruction.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 strongly supports the agency side of the ledger: right effort requires desire, striving, energy, applied mind, and maintenance across wholesome and unwholesome states. Shinran's Letter to Senshin sharply resists that grammar by denying calculative working on the practicer's side and placing settlement in Other Power. The ledger is useful because it notices the contrast, but the phrase receiving capacity is not simply textual in Shinran. The text more nearly says that the decisive settling is not possessed by the practitioner. Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction, https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/longchen-rabjam/lama-yangtik-trekcho-instruction, supports the relocation pattern by rejecting contrived effort while still requiring recognition of what has been introduced, undistracted abiding, vows, devotion, renunciation, and post-meditation conduct.

audits.title: The Capacity Ledger of Gift and Effort
audits.unlike_statement: Unlike Engler, Upaya, Pudgalavada personhood debates, or the prior Lumenary minimum-self claim, this idea says the comparative unit is a five-field load distribution, and that effort, gift, direct introduction, and practice-realization can be tested by where they relocate receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration. Because this follows directly from an earlier Lumenary audit instruction, it is best treated as an extension.

anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Successful hybrids and dual-trained practitioners who switch methods smoothly without reported directional resistance.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence from candidate discovery to novel synthesis. The model is promising only if practitioner reports and manual coding show directional resistance beyond ordinary difficulty, teacher style, or conceptual disagreement.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a vipassana-trained practitioner can move into Advaita self-inquiry, devotional surrender, or Dzogchen recognition without attention persistently tracking impermanence, non-ownership, or analytic deconstruction, then grain may be a comparative description rather than a lived capacity structure. Chan, Zen, modern cross-training, and eclectic contemplative therapy are especially important anomaly classes.

claim_units: Preparatory capacities are shaped by the dissolution they serve.
The same named capacity, such as attention or self-observation, functions differently in Theravada, Advaita, Sufism, Pure Land, Dzogchen, and Soto Zen.
Capacity and dissolution are mutually conditioned rather than independently first.
The five ledger variables carry direction, not only level.
When a practitioner trained in one tradition's grain attempts another tradition's dissolution, the old grain should resist or distort the new practice.
Adverse contemplative effects should cluster by grain mismatch, not only by missing prerequisites or practice intensity.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Clients and therapists crossing modalities should show grain-specific resistance: CBT-trained clients may convert psychoanalytic free association into problem solving, somatic-therapy clients may treat cognitive restructuring as disembodiment, and IFS-trained clients may over-personify processes in exposure work. Successful integrative therapy should include explicit re-graining, not only adding techniques.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A therapeutic capacity such as attention to emotion is not neutral. CBT, psychoanalysis, Internal Family Systems, somatic therapy, and exposure therapy train attention toward different transformations, such as belief revision, transference interpretation, parts differentiation, nervous-system regulation, or fear extinction.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Therapeutic skills transfer without directional residue, or outcomes and failures are predicted only by alliance, diagnosis, and treatment dose with no benefit from prior-modality grain.

audits.exact_claim: Spiritual prerequisites are not neutral capacities later applied to insight. Attention, discrimination, trust, longing, self-observation, and integration are trained with a directional grain toward the specific dissolution a tradition seeks, and mismatched grain should predict distinctive resistance and harm patterns.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Vipassana-trained practitioners attempting Advaita self-inquiry should initially keep tracking change and non-ownership rather than resting in witness-recognition. Advaita-trained practitioners attempting vipassana should tend to seek a stable knower or background rather than evenly noting impermanence. Practitioners trained in non-attachment who enter devotional surrender should report affective restraint or suspicion of dependence.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should experience method-specific transfer resistance.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with dual-trained practitioners, coded before doctrinal explanation is invited.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report smooth switching with no directional pull, or reported resistance is fully explained by temperament, teacher instructions, language, or community identity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Vivekachudamani-style qualifications should define readiness in terms of the real versus unreal and longing for liberation. Satipatthana and later insight manuals should define readiness through impermanence, suffering, non-ownership, and ethical abandonment. Sufi manuals should orient self-knowledge toward creaturely dependence before God. Dzogchen manuals should orient preparation toward devotion, introduction, recognition, and non-distraction.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Primary manuals should grain prerequisites toward their final dissolution.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding of Visuddhimagga chapter 1, Vivekachudamani vv. 17-30, Cloud of Unknowing chapters 3-6, al-Qushayri's Risala, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, and Satipatthana-related materials.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Prerequisites are generic across manuals, such as calm, attention, ethics, and perseverance, with little relation to the final insight language of each path.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Witness-trained practitioners pushed into rapid de-identification should show witness reification more than dissociation. Non-attachment-trained practitioners attempting devotional surrender should show emotional flattening more than spiritual inflation. Devotional practitioners entering analytic deconstruction should show relational rupture or fear of betrayal more than abstract nihilism.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Adverse effects should cluster by grain mismatch, not only by intensity, trauma history, or lack of prerequisites.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Re-coding VCE-style interview data, retreat adverse-event reports, and teacher case consultations for prior training, current method, failure type, and interpretive frame.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Adverse experiences cluster only by retreat intensity, sleep disruption, psychiatric history, social isolation, or teacher support, with no added explanatory value from prior practice grain.

literature_search_queries: Steven Katz Language Epistemology and Mysticism conceptual scheme shapes mystical experience
Robert Sharf Buddhist modernism rhetoric meditative experience bare attention mindfulness Buddhist
John Dunne Buddhist Styles of Mindfulness ethics judgment memory classical nondual
Jorge Ferrer participatory spirituality practices enact religious worlds
Lindahl Britton varieties contemplative experience adverse meditation interpretation practice context
dual trained practitioners Advaita Buddhism vipassana self inquiry practice transfer

near_neighbors.difference: The grain model narrows from experience content to the trained capacity that makes experience or dissolution possible.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The anti-neutrality claim is well known, though the prerequisite-specific formulation has room.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close background structure. Katz argues that mystical experience is shaped by memory, language, expectations, concepts, and tradition-specific frameworks.
near_neighbors.source: Steven T. Katz, Language, Epistemology, and Mysticism, summarized in Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, Mysticism, https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/mysticism/

near_neighbors.difference: Dunne is intra-Buddhist and practice-style focused. The grain model generalizes across traditions and claims capacities are aimed toward specific dissolutions and failure patterns.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction, especially for the claim that attention and mindfulness are not neutral tools.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Dunne distinguishes Classical and Nondual Buddhist styles and shows that mindfulness practices differ in ethics, judgment, present-centered awareness, memory, and theoretical coherence.
near_neighbors.source: John D. Dunne, Buddhist Styles of Mindfulness: A Heuristic Approach, https://www.johnddunne.net/uploads/9/8/5/6/9856107/dunne_j_mindfulness_heuristic.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: Sharf is primarily critical and historical. The grain model is constructive and predictive about prerequisite direction and mismatch harms.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the non-neutral-practice claim, moderate preservation for the predictive grain-mismatch thesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Sharf challenges generic meditative experience and generic bare attention, placing practice in historical, ritual, doctrinal, and institutional contexts.
near_neighbors.source: Robert H. Sharf, Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience, Deep Blue metadata, https://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/items/25b7e9d4-d1f3-4280-8d53-95fa0b53db5c, and Is mindfulness Buddhist?, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25361692/

near_neighbors.difference: Ferrer frames plural religious worlds and participatory events. The grain model focuses on prerequisite structure and the directional shaping of capacities before dissolution.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Participatory theory holds that religious phenomena are cocreated by human cognition, practice, community, and spiritual reality, rather than discovered as a neutral common core.
near_neighbors.source: Jorge Ferrer and colleagues, The Participatory Turn, SUNY Press, https://sunypress.edu/Books/T/The-Participatory-Turn2

near_neighbors.difference: The grain model expands from attentional instruments to all prerequisite capacities and adds a specific adverse-effect prediction.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High internal reduction. Grain is a meaningful development, not an independent discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact internal neighbor. It already says contemplative instruments are not separable from doctrine, and that Advaita self-inquiry and vipassana may produce different structural changes masked by coarse reports.
near_neighbors.source: Local Lumenary prior, The Instrument Problem: Attentional Divergence as an Epistemic Variable in Contemplative Convergence, observations/claude/2026-05-25-the-instrument-problem-attentional-divergence-as-an-epistemic-variable-in-contemplative-convergence.md

near_neighbors.difference: VCE does not claim capacities are directionally pre-shaped toward specific dissolutions or that mismatch predicts specific failures such as witness reification versus emotional flatness.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction and a strong test bed.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close empirical-adjacent source. VCE shows that similar meditation-related phenomenology may be interpreted, managed, and valenced differently by tradition, practice, teacher relation, and practitioner factors.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., The Varieties of Contemplative Experience, PLOS ONE, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

near_neighbors.difference: Capacity Ledger scores load placement. Grain adds directionality within each load and predicts resistance when a trained direction is repurposed.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high internal reduction. Grain may be the more specific synthesis, but it depends on the same architecture.
near_neighbors.overlap: Internal same-run neighbor. Both ideas use the five capacity variables and compare effort, gift, direct introduction, and practice-realization.
near_neighbors.source: The Capacity Ledger of Gift and Effort, observations/codex/2026-05-26-the-capacity-ledger-of-gift-and-effort.md

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as candidate discovery until dual-trained practitioner reports show directional resistance that cannot be reduced to doctrine, teacher style, or temperament.
Build a manual-coding rubric that separates capacity level from capacity direction, and code whether the direction is explicit in the primary text or inferred from the tradition's final aim.
Treat successful hybrids, smooth cross-training, and Pure Land other-power as anomaly classes, not supporting examples.
Search Hadot on spiritual exercises, Bourdieu on habitus, Sharf, Dunne, Katz, Ferrer, Forman, Taves, and VCE literature before raising novelty.
Run a small practitioner test with at least one teacher each from Theravada, Advaita, Dzogchen or Mahamudra, Sufism, and Pure Land, asking whether grain changes practice sequencing.
audits.novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.76 to about 0.58. The phrase grain of capacity is generative and the mismatch prediction is useful, but the anti-neutrality structure is strongly anticipated by constructivism, Buddhist mindfulness-style scholarship, participatory theory, adverse meditation research, and local Lumenary attentional-instrument work.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners and scholars say this is just Katz constructivism, Sharf on bare attention, Dunne's mindfulness styles, Ferrer's participatory pluralism, Engler's prerequisites, or ordinary lineage pedagogy. Dual-trained practitioners report no distinctive resistance beyond language and teacher expectations.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can name repeatable, tradition-specific transfer frictions and failure modes that the grain model predicts before doctrinal explanation, and teachers say the rubric improves sequencing and warnings for cross-training students.
questions: When you switch methods, does your trained attention keep moving in the old direction, or is the switch mostly effortless?
Is this idea already obvious as constructivism, practice style, Upaya, adhikara, or teacher pedagogy?
Would naming grain change how you sequence cross-training or diagnose practice failure?
relevant_practitioners: Comparative religion scholars of mysticism and practice

primary_pair: MN 10 Satipatthana Sutta and SN 22.59 Anattalakkhana Sutta, https://suttacentral.net/mn10/en/nyanamoli-thera and https://suttacentral.net/sn22.59/en/bodhi
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model may infer final-directional grain from a comparative observer's map rather than from practitioner experience. It also risks using a Madhyamaka fire-and-fuel lens as if all traditions accept dependent co-arising between capacity and dissolution. In Advaita or Sufi frames, sequence, grace, or revelation may be more important than co-arising.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search found strong near-neighbors in Katz's constructivism, Sharf on meditative experience and bare attention, Dunne on Buddhist styles of mindfulness, Ferrer's participatory theory, Hadot-style spiritual exercises, Engler's developmental prerequisite thesis, Lindahl and Britton's VCE work, and local Lumenary work on attentional instruments and verification architecture. I did not find the exact phrase grain of capacity or a specific claim that prerequisites, rather than experiences, are directionally carved toward the dissolution they enable. Still, most structural components are known.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Vivekachudamani strongly supports the grain claim because its prerequisites are already defined by the Advaita target: discrimination is framed as conviction that Brahman is real and the world is unreal, and calmness is tied to resting the mind on that goal. MN 10 is less explicit if read alone: it instructs body, feeling, mind, and dhamma contemplation with mindfulness and clear comprehension, but the opening formula does not itself say every act is aimed at non-ownership. SN 22.59 supplies that direction by repeatedly using impermanence, suffering, and non-mastery to reject mine, I, and self. The close reading therefore supports a weaker claim: some traditions explicitly grain prerequisites toward their final insight, while in others the grain must be established by reading practice instructions together with broader doctrinal and soteriological frames.

audits.title: The Grain of Capacity
audits.unlike_statement: Unlike Katz, Sharf, Dunne, Ferrer, Engler, and the local attentional-instrument thesis, this idea says that prerequisites themselves carry a directional grain toward the transformation they enable, and that this grain should predict specific transfer resistance and adverse effects when a practitioner crosses traditions.
